macro_summary: Mega-cap tech earnings season opens in earnest with TSMC reporting today, setting the AI capex demand tone for the sector. Fed rate hike probability collapsed from ~46% to below 17% after a soft June CPI print (-0.4% MoM), creating a macro tailwind for growth and tech into the July 29 FOMC decision. MSFT and AAPL both report July 29-30, making the final two weeks of July a concentrated dual-catalyst window. AI infrastructure demand — chips, cloud, memory — remains the dominant fundamental theme across overnight screening.
screening_note: 6 total overnight screening runs analyzed. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 6 qualifying tickers met or exceeded this threshold. Ideas ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: TSM Q2 2026 earnings today July 16 at ~2:00 PM ET — consensus $3.83 EPS (+55% YoY), Q3 guide is the swing factor
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29 after close — Azure AI ROI and Copilot monetization in focus
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30 after close — China AI (Qwen integration) approval confirmed, iPhone 18 cycle ahead
Fed FOMC rate decision July 29 — hike probability now below 17% after soft CPI
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26 — CEO dismissed Vera Rubin delay reports July 15
SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) freshly listed July 10; Barclays Overweight initiation with ~$330 PT (~77% upside); Q2 earnings late July

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens with high conviction — the strongest consensus signal in this digest. The stock is down ~20% YTD and trades at ~20x forward earnings vs. its historical ~30x average, representing a ~50% re-rating gap that a beat-and-raise on July 29 could begin to close. The bar is deliberately low: Q4 consensus EPS of $4.24 is actually below Q3's $4.27 print, and the July 29 date coincides with the FOMC decision, amplifying the macro tailwind if the Fed holds.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 after close; same-day FOMC decision
ideas.edge: Trading at ~20x forward P/E vs. historical ~30x average — a ~50% re-rating gap; analyst consensus PT ~$589–$592 implies 56% upside from current price; EPS bar of $4.24 is below last quarter's $4.27 actual
ideas.bull_case: A beat-and-raise on Azure growth and Copilot monetization unlocks a multiple re-rating from cheapest-since-2023 levels; Fed hold on the same day removes the macro overhang and accelerates the move.
ideas.bear_case: Azure capex ROI questions persist; if cloud growth decelerates or guidance disappoints, the compressed multiple offers limited protection and a miss could extend the YTD drawdown.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL also appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens and hit an all-time high on July 15 (+4% session), with momentum stacking into the July 30 earnings print. Three distinct catalysts are now live simultaneously: (1) China AI regulatory approval for Qwen/Apple Intelligence integration unlocking a re-acceleration in the world's largest iPhone market, (2) Citi PT raised to $365 from $315 implying ~16% further upside, and (3) the highest revenue guidance range (14–17% growth) in years, building on Q2's 22% iPhone YoY jump. The ~$4.56T market cap is within 4% of reclaiming the world's most valuable company title.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; China AI (Qwen integration) approval already confirmed
ideas.edge: Citi PT raised to $365 (~16% upside from prior close); stock hit ATH July 15; Q3 guidance of 14–17% revenue growth with prior quarter iPhone sales +22% YoY; fresh $100B buyback program active
ideas.bull_case: China AI approval re-opens the largest growth market, iPhone 18 cycle and foldable iPhone catalysts layer on top of already strong Services revenue — a triple-catalyst setup into the print with momentum at its back.
ideas.bear_case: Entering at all-time highs raises the expectations bar; any disappointment on China sell-through data or Services margins could trigger a sharp reversal given stretched positioning and elevated market cap.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with mixed but constructive conviction. The stock trades at ~22x forward P/E on 85% revenue growth and 75% gross margins — a PEG of ~0.6 that screens as historically cheap for this growth profile. Today's TSMC Q2 earnings report is an immediate same-day read-through on AI chip demand, and CEO Jensen Huang dismissed Vera Rubin hardware delay reports on July 15, removing the most prominent near-term bear narrative. The August 26 earnings date is the next major re-rating event.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC Q2 earnings today July 16 (direct demand read-through); NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026 after close; AI export restriction easing confirmed
ideas.edge: 68-analyst consensus PT of ~$302.73 implies ~49% upside from ~$210; trades ~11% below 52-week high of $236.54; forward P/E ~22x on 85% revenue growth = PEG ~0.6; revenue estimate revised up 6.69% in past 3 months
ideas.bull_case: A TSMC beat today confirms peak AI chip demand, Vera Rubin delay fears are now officially dismissed by management, and the August 26 print gives time for August call spreads to appreciate as the setup builds.
ideas.bear_case: Profit-taking and sector rotation have pressured the stock recently; if TSMC guidance disappoints or macro risk-off accelerates on Iran escalation, NVDA could see further near-term weakness before the August catalyst.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens as the most direct US-listed pure-play on AI HBM memory scarcity, freshly listed on Nasdaq July 10. Barclays initiated with an Overweight and $330 PT against a current price of ~$186 — a ~77% upside gap. The structural thesis is reinforced by SK Hynix's own CEO forecasting the worst-ever HBM supply shortage in 2027 with shortages potentially lasting beyond 2030, while NVDA's AI chip demand creates captive HBM demand. Q2 earnings expected late July will be the first major fundamental data point for the ADR.
ideas.catalyst: Barclays Overweight initiation July 14 ($330 PT); SK Hynix Q2 earnings expected late July; South Korea record AI budget announced; CEO HBM shortage warning
ideas.edge: Barclays PT $330 vs. ~$186 current = ~77% upside; ADR IPO at $149, now $186 — still well below analyst target; DRAM/HBM prices projected +50% on structural shortage; total revenue +40.82% YoY; options ecosystem now live
ideas.bull_case: HBM supply shortage is structural and multi-year per company's own CEO; Barclays target gap is substantial; freshly listed ADR with rising US institutional interest and the only liquid US-listed pure-play HBM vehicle.
ideas.bear_case: ADR trades at a 51% premium to the underlying Korean shares — a significant structural overhang; post-IPO volatility is extreme and options liquidity remains thin; any Korea-specific risk or USD/KRW move could compress the premium sharply.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: TSM appeared in 2 of 6 overnight screens — at the minimum threshold — but with high conviction both times, and today's earnings report makes this the most time-sensitive idea in the digest. TSMC has beaten estimates in each of the last 4 quarters with an average positive surprise of 8.34%, and the Q3 guidance update is the most anticipated forward-looking AI capex read-through of the season. June revenue already came in +68% YoY, providing a strong leading indicator into the Q2 print. A beat-and-raise today would act as a rising tide for NVDA, ASML, and the broader semis complex.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report today July 16 ~2:00 PM ET; Q3 2026 revenue guidance update imminent
ideas.edge: 4-quarter average earnings beat of 8.34%; June revenue +68% YoY pre-reported; Barclays PT raised to $625 from $470; full-year 2026 revenue growth guided >30% in USD terms; consensus Q2 EPS $3.83 vs $2.47 YoY (+55%)
ideas.bull_case: A beat on Q2 and strong Q3 guide confirming AI chip demand acceleration would be the most powerful positive sector catalyst of earnings season, re-rating TSMC and lifting the entire semis supply chain.
ideas.bear_case: Geopolitical Taiwan risk is perpetually elevated; any Q3 guidance softness or cautious commentary on CoWoS/advanced packaging capacity would disappoint a market priced for perfection; options capture defined risk around the binary print.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 2 of 6 screens at the minimum threshold, flagged primarily as a macro hedge and directional vehicle around the July 29 dual-catalyst date — MSFT earnings and the FOMC decision on the same afternoon. Rate hike probability collapsed from ~46% to below 17% after June CPI came in -0.4% MoM, creating a macro tailwind for the Nasdaq-100. With VIX at ~15.95, option premiums are relatively affordable for capturing either the upside continuation or a tail-risk hedge around the concentrated two-week earnings window.
ideas.catalyst: Fed FOMC rate decision July 29, 2026; MSFT earnings July 29 after close; AAPL earnings July 30 — compressed dual-catalyst window
ideas.edge: Rate hike probability dropped from ~46% to <17% on soft June CPI; VIX ~15.95 keeps option premiums reasonable; mega-cap earnings cluster July 29-30 creates concentrated optionality window
ideas.bull_case: Fed holds + MSFT/AAPL beat-and-raise scenario creates a powerful synchronized rally in the two largest Nasdaq-100 components, lifting QQQ to new highs with near-term momentum.
ideas.bear_case: A surprise Fed hike on July 29 combined with any earnings disappointment from MSFT or AAPL would compress tech multiples simultaneously — a QQQ put spread or strangle captures this tail risk cleanly.
